p: Welcome to the documentation for capmonster_python — a modern, fully typed, async-ready Python SDK for Capmonster.Cloud.
Integrate with 20+ CAPTCHA challenge types including reCAPTCHA, Turnstile, GeeTest, FunCaptcha, Image-to-Text, and more — with both sync and async support out of the box.
Installation — Get up and running in seconds
Examples — Copy-paste code for every task type
API Reference — Full parameter docs for all classes
solve() convenience method — create + poll in one call
Task abstraction for all Capmonster types
Prosopo, Basilisk, FunCaptcha , MTCaptcha , Yidun , Altcha , Castle , TSPD
V2, V2 Enterprise, V3, V3 Enterprise
This library is actively maintained and tracks the latest Capmonster Cloud API. It targets Python 3.9+ and is distributed under the MIT license.
This project is an unofficial, community-maintained SDK. It is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or supported by ZennoLab or Capmonster.Cloud.
The authors of this library make no guarantees about the availability, accuracy, or reliability of the Capmonster Cloud service itself. Use at your own risk.

By using this library you agree to comply with the Capmonster Cloud Terms of Service. The authors accept no liability for any damages, account restrictions, or other consequences arising from its use.

c: pip install capmonster_python
pipx install capmonster_python
poetry add capmonster_python
pip install capmonster-python==3.2

p: Below are example usages for various task types, shown in both async and sync format using CapmonsterClient.
Using a context manager ensures HTTP connections are properly closed when you're done.
c: import asyncio from capmonster_python import CapmonsterClient, RecaptchaV2Task async def main(): async with CapmonsterClient(api_key="<API_KEY>") as client: task = RecaptchaV2Task( websiteURL="https://example.com", websiteKey="<site_key>" ) task_id = await client.create_task_async(task=task) result = await client.get_task_result_async(task_id=task_id) print(result) asyncio.run(main())

from capmonster_python import CapmonsterClient, RecaptchaV2Task with CapmonsterClient(api_key="<API_KEY>") as client: task = RecaptchaV2Task( websiteURL="https://example.com", websiteKey="<site_key>" ) task_id = client.create_task(task=task) result = client.get_task_result(task_id=task_id) print(result)

p: The solve() method combines create_task + join_task_result into a single call.
c: import asyncio from capmonster_python import CapmonsterClient, RecaptchaV2Task async def main(): client = CapmonsterClient(api_key="<API_KEY>") result = await client.solve_async(RecaptchaV2Task( websiteURL="https://example.com", websiteKey="<site_key>" )) print(result.get("gRecaptchaResponse")) asyncio.run(main())

from capmonster_python import CapmonsterClient, RecaptchaV2Task client = CapmonsterClient(api_key="<API_KEY>") result = client.solve(RecaptchaV2Task( websiteURL="https://example.com", websiteKey="<site_key>" )) print(result.get("gRecaptchaResponse"))

p: Customize the polling behavior when waiting for task results with max_retries and retry_delay.
c: import asyncio from capmonster_python import CapmonsterClient, RecaptchaV2Task async def main(): client = CapmonsterClient( api_key="<API_KEY>", max_retries=60, retry_delay=2.0 ) result = await client.solve_async(RecaptchaV2Task( websiteURL="https://example.com", websiteKey="<site_key>" )) print(result) asyncio.run(main())

from capmonster_python import CapmonsterClient, RecaptchaV2Task client = CapmonsterClient( api_key="<API_KEY>", max_retries=60, retry_delay=2.0 ) result = client.solve(RecaptchaV2Task( websiteURL="https://example.com", websiteKey="<site_key>" )) print(result)

import asyncio from capmonster_python import CapmonsterClient, RecaptchaV2Task async def main(): client = CapmonsterClient(api_key="<API_KEY>") task = RecaptchaV2Task( websiteURL="https://example.com", websiteKey="<site_key>", isInvisible=False ) task_id = await client.create_task_async(task=task) result = await client.get_task_result_async(task_id=task_id) print(result.get("gRecaptchaResponse")) asyncio.run(main())

from capmonster_python import CapmonsterClient, RecaptchaV2Task client = CapmonsterClient(api_key="<API_KEY>") task_id = client.create_task(task=RecaptchaV2Task( websiteURL="https://example.com", websiteKey="<site_key>", isInvisible=False )) response = client.get_task_result(task_id=task_id) print(response.get("gRecaptchaResponse"))

p: Used for solving Google reCAPTCHA V2 Enterprise challenges. Optional support for enterprisePayload, custom apiDomain, and cookies.
c: import asyncio from capmonster_python import CapmonsterClient, RecaptchaV2EnterpriseTask async def main(): client = CapmonsterClient(api_key="<API_KEY>") task = RecaptchaV2EnterpriseTask( websiteURL="https://example.com", websiteKey="<site_key>", enterprisePayload="{\"s\":\"abc123\"}", cookies="cookie=value;cookie_2=value2" ) task_id = await client.create_task_async(task) result = await client.get_task_result_async(task_id) print(result.get("gRecaptchaResponse")) asyncio.run(main())

from capmonster_python import CapmonsterClient, RecaptchaV2EnterpriseTask client = CapmonsterClient(api_key="<API_KEY>") task_id = client.create_task(RecaptchaV2EnterpriseTask( websiteURL="https://example.com", websiteKey="<site_key>", enterprisePayload="{\"s\":\"abc123\"}", cookies="cookie=value;cookie_2=value2" )) result = client.get_task_result(task_id) print(result.get("gRecaptchaResponse"))

p: Used for solving score-based reCAPTCHA v3 challenges without requiring a proxy. You can optionally provide minScore and pageAction to match expected interaction patterns.
c: import asyncio from capmonster_python import CapmonsterClient, RecaptchaV3Task async def main(): client = CapmonsterClient(api_key="<API_KEY>") task = RecaptchaV3Task( websiteURL="https://example.com", websiteKey="<site_key>", minScore=0.5, pageAction="verify" ) task_id = await client.create_task_async(task) result = await client.get_task_result_async(task_id) print(result.get("gRecaptchaResponse")) asyncio.run(main())

from capmonster_python import CapmonsterClient, RecaptchaV3Task client = CapmonsterClient(api_key="<API_KEY>") task_id = client.create_task(RecaptchaV3Task( websiteURL="https://example.com", websiteKey="<site_key>", minScore=0.5, pageAction="verify" )) result = client.get_task_result(task_id) print(result.get("gRecaptchaResponse"))

import asyncio from capmonster_python import CapmonsterClient, ImageToTextTask async def main(): client = CapmonsterClient(api_key="<API_KEY>") task = ImageToTextTask( body="<base64-image>", recognizingThreshold=95 ) task_id = await client.create_task_async(task=task) result = await client.get_task_result_async(task_id=task_id) print(result.get("text")) asyncio.run(main())

from capmonster_python import CapmonsterClient, ImageToTextTask client = CapmonsterClient(api_key="<API_KEY>") task = ImageToTextTask( body="<base64-image>", recognizingThreshold=95 ) task_id = client.create_task(task=task) response = client.get_task_result(task_id=task_id) print(response.get("text"))

import asyncio from capmonster_python import CapmonsterClient, GeeTestV3Task async def main(): client = CapmonsterClient(api_key="<API_KEY>") task = GeeTestV3Task( websiteURL="https://example.com", gt="<gt-value>", challenge="<challenge-value>" ) task_id = await client.create_task_async(task) result = await client.get_task_result_async(task_id) print(result) asyncio.run(main())

from capmonster_python import CapmonsterClient, GeeTestV3Task client = CapmonsterClient(api_key="<API_KEY>") task_id = client.create_task(GeeTestV3Task( websiteURL="https://example.com", gt="<gt-value>", challenge="<challenge-value>" )) result = client.get_task_result(task_id) print(result)

p: Used for solving advanced GeeTest v4 captchas which require both gt and initParameters. The challenge is generated dynamically per user and must be fresh.
c: import asyncio from capmonster_python import CapmonsterClient, GeeTestV4Task async def main(): client = CapmonsterClient(api_key="<API_KEY>") task = GeeTestV4Task( websiteURL="https://example.com", gt="<gt-value>", initParameters={"riskType": "slide"} ) task_id = await client.create_task_async(task) result = await client.get_task_result_async(task_id) print(result) asyncio.run(main())

from capmonster_python import CapmonsterClient, GeeTestV4Task client = CapmonsterClient(api_key="<API_KEY>") task_id = client.create_task(GeeTestV4Task( websiteURL="https://example.com", gt="<gt-value>", initParameters={"riskType": "slide"} )) result = client.get_task_result(task_id) print(result)

p: Used for bypassing complex Cloudflare protection involving cf_clearance cookie and HTML snapshot.
c: import asyncio from capmonster_python import CapmonsterClient, TurnstileCloudFlareTask, ProxyPayload async def main(): client = CapmonsterClient(api_key="<API_KEY>") task = TurnstileCloudFlareTask( cloudflareTaskType="cf_clearance", websiteURL="https://example.com", websiteKey="<website_key>", userAgent="Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64)...", htmlPageBase64="<base64-encoded-page>", proxy=ProxyPayload( proxyType="https", proxyAddress="192.168.1.1", proxyPort=8000) ) task_id = await client.create_task_async(task) result = await client.get_task_result_async(task_id) print(result) asyncio.run(main())

from capmonster_python import CapmonsterClient, TurnstileCloudFlareTask, ProxyPayload client = CapmonsterClient(api_key="<API_KEY>") task = TurnstileCloudFlareTask( cloudflareTaskType="cf_clearance", websiteURL="https://example.com", websiteKey="<website_key>", userAgent="Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64)...", htmlPageBase64="<base64-encoded-page>", proxy=ProxyPayload( proxyType="https", proxyAddress="192.168.1.1", proxyPort=8000) ) task_id = client.create_task(task=task) result = client.get_task_result(task_id) print(result)

p: Used specifically for login flows involving Binance CAPTCHA protection. You must supply a valid validateId (such as securityId, validateId, or similar) retrieved from the page.

p: Welcome to the Capmonster Python API reference.

This section provides detailed documentation for all available task types and core classes in the Capmonster ecosystem. Whether you're solving reCAPTCHA, Turnstile, or advanced behavioral CAPTCHAs like DataDome or Imperva, this reference will guide you through the available models, parameters, and behaviors.
Capmonster Python v4 is fully typed with Pydantic and exposes task models via clear class structures. All classes include:
to_request() methods for raw dict generation
Context manager — use with / async with to auto-close HTTP connections
solve() / solve_async() — convenience method combining create_task + join_task_result
Configurable polling — set max_retries and retry_delay on CapmonsterClient
9 new task types — FunCaptcha, Amazon WAF, reCAPTCHA v3 Enterprise, Cloudflare Waiting Room, MTCaptcha, Yidun, Altcha, Castle, TSPD
All tasks are instantiable Pydantic models
If you are solving proxy-based CAPTCHAs, configure proxy and userAgent attributes
Task classes do not make API calls — use them with CapmonsterClient

These are the base classes used internally to build all task payloads. You typically don't instantiate them directly, but they are useful for understanding the type hierarchy and for building custom tasks via VanillaTaskPayload.
Abstract base model for all task payloads. Every task type inherits from this class.
The task type identifier sent to the Capmonster API (e.g. RecaptchaV2Task).

c: class TaskPayload(BaseModel, ABC)

p: Abstract method. Converts the task instance into a dictionary suitable for the API request body.
IPv4/IPv6 proxy address. Hostnames and local proxies are not allowed.
Proxy configuration model. Used by tasks that support or require proxy settings.

c: class ProxyPayload(BaseModel)

from capmonster_python import ProxyPayload

proxy = ProxyPayload(
    proxyType="https",
    proxyAddress="192.168.1.1",
    proxyPort=8080,
    proxyLogin="user",
    proxyPassword="pass"
)

p: Mixin base class that adds an optional userAgent field to task payloads.
Browser User-Agent string used for captcha recognition.

c: class UserAgentPayload(BaseModel, ABC)

p: Forward-compatible base class for custom or newly introduced task types. Allows extra fields via Pydantic's model_config = {extra: allow}.
Use this as a base to quickly integrate a new task format not yet supported by the SDK:

c: class VanillaTaskPayload(TaskPayload, UserAgentPayload)

from capmonster_python import VanillaTaskPayload

class CustomCaptchaTask(VanillaTaskPayload):
    type: str = "NewCustomTask"
    custom_field: str

The main client for interacting with the Capmonster Cloud API. Supports both synchronous and asynchronous usage, context manager protocol, and configurable polling.
Delay in seconds between polling attempts.
Maximum number of polling attempts in join_task_result / solve.

c: CapmonsterClient(api_key: str, timeout: float = 30.0, max_retries: int = 120, retry_delay: float = 2.0)

p: CapmonsterClient supports both sync and async context managers to ensure HTTP connections are properly closed.
c: with CapmonsterClient(api_key="YOUR_KEY") as client: result = client.solve(task)
async with CapmonsterClient(api_key="YOUR_KEY") as client: result = await client.solve_async(task)

p: Returns: float — The account balance. Defaults to 0.0 if not found.

c:
def get_balance(self) -> float
async def get_balance_async(self) -> float

p: Creates a captcha-solving task and returns the task ID.
Optional URL to be called on task completion.
Returns: int — The unique task identifier.
Raises: CapmonsterException if the request fails or the response contains no valid task ID.

c:
def create_task(self, task: TaskPayload, callback_url: str | None = None) -> int
async def create_task_async(self, task: TaskPayload, callback_url: str | None = None) -> int

p: Fetches the result of a completed task (single poll, no waiting).
The task identifier returned by create_task.
Returns: dict — The solution dictionary, or {} if the task is not yet ready.

c:
def get_task_result(self, task_id: int) -> dict
async def get_task_result_async(self, task_id: int) -> dict

p: Polls for the task result until it is ready, up to max_retries attempts with retry_delay between each.
The task identifier returned by create_task.
Returns: dict — The solution dictionary from the completed task.
Raises: CapmonsterAPIException with code ERROR_MAXIMUM_TIME_EXCEED if polling exhausts all retries.

c:
def join_task_result(self, task_id: int) -> dict
async def join_task_result_async(self, task_id: int) -> dict

p: Convenience method that creates a task and polls for its result in one call. Equivalent to create_task() followed by join_task_result().
Optional callback URL for task completion.
Returns: dict — The solution dictionary from the completed task.

c:
def solve(self, task: TaskPayload, callback_url: str | None = None) -> dict
async def solve_async(self, task: TaskPayload, callback_url: str | None = None) -> dict

p: Reports an incorrect image captcha solution.
Raises: CapmonsterAPIException if the API returns an error.

c:
def report_incorrect_image(self, task_id: int) -> None
async def report_incorrect_image_async(self, task_id: int) -> None

p: Reports an incorrect token captcha solution (reCAPTCHA, GeeTest, Turnstile, etc.).
Raises: CapmonsterAPIException if the API returns an error.

c:
def report_incorrect_token(self, task_id: int) -> None
async def report_incorrect_token_async(self, task_id: int) -> None

p: Fetches the current valid Windows User-Agent string from CapMonster Cloud.
Returns: str — The current User-Agent string to use with captcha tasks.

c:
def get_user_agent(self) -> str
async def get_user_agent_async(self) -> str

This page documents common error codes returned by the CapMonster Cloud API along with their meanings and possible solutions.
Invalid API Key The authorization key provided is not recognized or is in the wrong format.
Check your API key from the CapMonster Dashboard.
No Funds Your account balance is zero.
Add credits to continue solving captchas.
IP Not Allowed Requests from your current IP address are not allowed.
Add your IP to the trusted list in your account settings.
IP Banned Too many failed requests with incorrect API key from your IP.
Verify your key, wait a few minutes, and try again.
Your IP is Blocked Your IP is permanently blocked due to repeated violations or suspicious activity.
Captcha Image Too Large The image size exceeds 500 KB.
Compress or resize the image before submitting.
Captcha Image Too Small The image size is less than 100 bytes.
Ensure the image is valid and properly encoded.
Captcha Unsolvable The image may be too noisy, corrupted, or unsupported by the service.
Try another image or adjust your task.
Captcha ID Not Found No task found for the given ID, or the request was made after it expired.
Poll results within 5 minutes of task creation.
Captcha Not Ready The task is still being processed.
ReCAPTCHA Timeout Task solving took too long, possibly due to slow proxy or server issues.
Use a faster proxy or adjust timeout settings.
Domain Blocked CapMonster Cloud does not support solving captchas for the specified domain.
Use a different domain or provider.
Invalid SiteKey The specified websiteKey is not valid.
Double-check the data-sitekey on the target page.
Invalid Domain The domain doesn't match the sitekey's origin.
Ensure both belong to the same origin.
Token Expired The challenge token has expired.
Create a new task with a fresh token.
Incorrect Method The specified task type (in type) is invalid or unsupported.
Incorrect Task Type The task format is not recognized.
Review the type value and match it to the correct schema.
Missing Task Object No task object was provided, or the JSON is malformed.
Invalid User-Agent The specified User-Agent string is no longer accepted.
Update to a current, valid browser UA string.
Proxy Connection Failed Unable to reach proxy server.
Test your proxy or replace it.
Proxy Banned The proxy IP is blocked by the target captcha provider.
Rotate your proxy or try a different provider.
Too Many Result Polls You polled for the task result too frequently.
Wait at least 2 seconds between requests.
No Free Servers All recognition servers are currently busy.
These error codes are related to Capmonster.cloud and not created by us.
You can reach the latest version from here.
API usage has completely changed from v3.2 to v4.0. All task creation methods now rely on structured payloads and typed clients.
full rewrite with async support, pydantic v2 and modern architecture (#72) (bd9e40d)
add features section to documentation index (17efb36)
You can find other version changelogs here.
